text:
Market today: Mixed/cautious mood. S&P 500 flagged at a critical crossroads per headlines, and Fed's Goolsbee keeping inflation concerns alive — that combo keeps rate-cut hopes muted and pressure on high-multiple tech names like yours.

Your holdings: PLTR is the loudest alarm — down 17.6% and scanner says SELL; on margin that hole keeps growing if it slides toward your $85 stop. AVGO is your largest position, down 13.2% and flagged TRIM — size plus loss on margin is a real risk. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history — tiny moves can be violent; margin amplifies every tick against you.

Watch: 1) Whether SPY breaks its current level cleanly — headline flags a critical crossroads, and a leg lower drags your whole book. 2) Any fresh Fed commentary — Goolsbee vs. Williams disagreement means rate narrative is unsettled, which whips tech.

Bottom line: Your two biggest problems are PLTR (act on the SELL signal) and AVGO's size — trim both before the market decides for you.
